meta_title: Roofing in Hollywood, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Hollywood, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems built for HVHZ. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates.
h1: Hollywood's Trusted Roofing Experts — Built for the HVHZ, Built to Last
intro:
Situated just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coast, Hollywood, Florida is one of Broward County's most beloved communities — and one of its most storm-exposed. From the bustling Hollywood Beach Broadwalk to the quiet tree-lined streets of Emerald Hills and the waterfront estates of Hollywood Lakes, homeowners here share a common challenge: keeping a roof overhead that can stand up to nature's most unforgiving conditions. With an average home value of $400,000 and a local real estate market that rewards well-maintained properties, your roof isn't just a functional necessity — it's one of the most valuable investments you can make in your home.

Hollywood sits squarely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), a designation reserved for the most hurricane-vulnerable regions of the state and enforced under the strictest construction standards in the nation. This means that roofing systems installed here must meet elevated performance benchmarks that go far beyond what's required in most of Florida. With sustained wind design speeds of 170 mph, the roofing products and installation methods approved for use in Hollywood are engineered to handle the full force of a major hurricane — and the consequences of cutting corners can be catastrophic and costly.

Approximately 55% of Hollywood's housing stock was built before 1994, predating the sweeping code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. Homes in Hollywood Hills, Downtown Hollywood, and neighborhoods throughout the 33019, 33020, and 33021 ZIP codes may be sitting under aging roofing systems that are simply not up to modern hurricane standards. Whether your roof shows visible signs of wear, has survived one too many storm seasons, or simply needs a code-compliant upgrade before your next insurance renewal, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is here to provide a seamless, expert solution — from the first inspection to the final permit sign-off.

section_why.h2: Why Hollywood Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've earned our reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by doing things the right way — every time. We are a fully licensed and insured South Florida roofing contractor with an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and more than 500 completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience to every job, and we understand that the unique demands of Hollywood's coastal environment require more than just showing up with materials and a crew.

One of the most important distinctions that sets us apart from other roofing companies in Broward County is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract your roof replacement to third-party crews whose quality we cannot guarantee. Every team member who works on your home is a trained Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who follows our strict installation protocols, understands HVHZ code requirements, and takes personal pride in delivering a finished product that will protect your home for decades. This matters especially in Hollywood, where the City of Hollywood Building Division enforces rigorous inspection standards.

We serve every corner of Hollywood — from the coastal residences near Hollywood Beach and the Anne Kolb Nature Center, to mid-city neighborhoods like Hollywood Hills and the growing Downtown Hollywood district near ArtsPark at Young Circle. We know the local architectural styles, we know what the inspectors look for, and we know how to get your project completed on schedule and on budget. Our familiarity with Broward County permitting timelines and the City of Hollywood Building Division's specific requirements means fewer delays and smoother project flow from start to finish.

Finally, we back our work with transparent communication and honest guidance. We'll walk you through your product options, explain the code requirements in plain language, help you understand your insurance eligibility, and provide a detailed written estimate with no hidden fees. Our financing options — starting from $87 per month with $0 down — make a code-compliant, hurricane-rated roof replacement accessible for middle-income Hollywood homeowners without sacrificing quality for cost.

section_history.h2: Hollywood's Storm History Makes Quality Roofing a Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Hollywood has faced more than 10 named storms since 1990, and the damage record tells a sobering story. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 fundamentally reshaped Florida's understanding of what hurricanes can do to residential structures — and the widespread roof failures it caused directly led to the HVHZ building code reforms that Hollywood now operates under. At the time, tens of thousands of South Florida roofs were ripped off or structurally compromised, exposing the critical gap between older construction standards and real-world storm performance.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of devastation to Broward County — rapidly changing pressure gradients, intense wind shear, and prolonged exposure that tested even newer roofing systems. Hollywood neighborhoods experienced significant shingle loss, ridge cap failures, and water intrusion damage in the weeks and months following the storm. And then in 2017, Hurricane Irma reminded the region that no storm season should be taken lightly. While Irma's most destructive winds fell south of Hollywood, the sustained tropical storm and early hurricane-force gusts caused widespread damage to aging roofs throughout the city, particularly in pre-1994 homes where original fastening patterns and underlayment systems were no longer adequate.

The cumulative effect of repeated storm exposure means that many Hollywood roofs — even those that appear visually intact — may have accumulated hidden damage: cracked or displaced tiles, compromised underlayment, weakened fastener pull-out resistance, and deteriorating flashings. A roof that survived Irma may not survive the next major storm. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's comprehensive roof assessments are designed to identify these hidden vulnerabilities and give Hollywood homeowners a clear, honest picture of their home's true level of protection.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Roofing Standards and Permit Requirements in Hollywood, Florida
section_code.body:
Hollywood, Florida falls entirely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which is defined under the Florida Building Code and carries the most stringent roofing requirements in the United States. The HVHZ encompasses Miami-Dade and Broward counties, and any roofing product or system installed within its boundaries must hold a Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) — a rigorous product approval process that tests materials under extreme wind uplift, impact, and water infiltration conditions. Only products that pass these tests are legally permitted for installation on Hollywood homes.

Under current Florida Building Code requirements, Hollywood roofing projects must also meet wind design speed requirements of 170 mph, reflecting the city's coastal exposure and proximity to open water. This means roofing assemblies must be designed and installed to resist calculated wind uplift pressures at the field, perimeter, and corner zones of the roof — not just attached and hoped for the best. Fastener type, fastener spacing, nail penetration depth, underlayment type, and deck attachment all factor into a compliant installation. These are not details that can be improvised on the job site.

All roofing replacements and major repairs in Hollywood require permits through the City of Hollywood Building Division, and permitted work must pass one or more inspections before the project can be closed out. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — we prepare and submit all documentation, schedule inspections, and ensure that every phase of your roof replacement meets the inspector's approval. This protects you legally, validates your warranty, and ensures that your insurance company cannot dispute coverage following a future storm event. Working with a properly permitted, HVHZ-compliant roofing contractor is not optional in Hollywood — it's the law, and it's the only way to truly protect your investment.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Series and West Lake Tile Systems — Engineered for Hollywood's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing product lines that are purpose-built for South Florida's demanding coastal and HVHZ environment: CertainTeed architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both brands hold the product approvals and Miami-Dade NOA certifications required for legal installation in Hollywood, and both are available in a range of styles and colors to complement the diverse architectural character of Hollywood's neighborhoods.

CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles represent the gold standard in asphalt shingle performance for South Florida homes. These dimensional shingles feature a multi-layer construction with enhanced fiberglass mat reinforcement, a durable SBS-modified asphalt formulation in select lines, and CertainTeed's proprietary algae-resistance technology — critical in Hollywood's humid, salt-air environment where black streaking can degrade a roof's appearance and performance within just a few years. The Landmark Series carries Class A fire ratings and is engineered to meet the wind resistance requirements of the HVHZ when installed per manufacturer specifications. For Hollywood homeowners looking for a cost-effective, low-maintenance, and visually attractive roofing solution, CertainTeed Landmark shingles deliver outstanding long-term value.

For Hollywood homes where tile roofing is either original to the home or preferred for aesthetic reasons, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer unmatched durability and storm resilience. Tile roofing has long been the dominant roofing material in South Florida's coastal communities, and for good reason — a properly installed tile system can last 40 to 50 years, withstand wind uplift forces that would destroy a standard shingle roof, and provide excellent thermal mass that helps reduce cooling costs in Hollywood's long, hot summers. West Lake's product line includes both concrete and authentic clay tile options in profiles ranging from classic barrel tile to flat and low-profile styles that complement Hollywood's mix of Mediterranean, ranch, and contemporary architecture.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Hollywood, FL — and How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
Roof replacement costs in Hollywood vary depending on the size of your home, the pitch and complexity of the roof, the material selected, and the scope of any necessary deck repairs or structural work discovered during installation. For a typical single-family home in Hollywood — where the average home value sits at approximately $400,000 — a complete roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingles generally ranges from $12,000 to $20,000, while a full West Lake tile roof replacement can range from $18,000 to $35,000 or more depending on tile profile and project complexity. These are investments, not expenses — and the return is substantial.

A modern, hurricane-rated roof can add meaningful value to your Hollywood home at resale, reduce your annual homeowner's insurance premium by hundreds or even thousands of dollars, and potentially save you from devastating out-of-pocket costs following a storm. When you factor in the insurance savings alone — which we'll detail in the next section — many Hollywood homeowners find that their new roof pays for itself in insurance premium reductions within 5 to 8 years. That's a compelling financial case for moving forward rather than deferring the investment.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes financing a new roof straightforward and accessible for middle-income Hollywood homeowners. We offer flexible financing options starting from as little as $87 per month with $0 down, allowing you to protect your home today without depleting your savings or waiting for a financial windfall. Our team will walk you through available financing programs, help you understand the projected insurance savings, and provide a full written estimate so you can make a confident, informed decision. There are no surprises and no pressure — just honest guidance from a team that's invested in your long-term satisfaction.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums in Hollywood
section_insurance.body:
Insurance costs in Broward County are among the highest in the nation, and Hollywood homeowners — particularly those in coastal ZIP codes like 33019 and 33020 — often bear some of the heaviest premium burdens. A primary driver of these elevated costs is the age and condition of the home's roof. Insurance carriers operating in Florida, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, HCI Group, and others, treat the roof as the single most critical factor in determining a home's vulnerability to hurricane damage — and they price their policies accordingly.

When you replace your roof with a code-compliant, HVHZ-rated system, you become eligible for a Wind Mitigation Inspection — a formal assessment of your home's hurricane resistance features conducted by a licensed inspector. This inspection documents your new roof's attributes, including its opening protection, deck attachment method, roof covering type, and roof-to-wall connection strength. The resulting Wind Mitigation Report is submitted to your insurance carrier, who then applies credits based on the documented features. In South Florida, these credits can reduce the wind portion of your homeowner's insurance premium by 20% to 45% or more — potentially saving Hollywood homeowners $1,500 to $4,000 or more annually depending on the carrier and the policy structure.

Beyond the wind mitigation credits, a new roof also eliminates one of the most common reasons that insurance companies drop or non-renew policies in Florida: an aging, non-compliant roof. Many Hollywood homeowners with pre-1994 roofs or roofs approaching 20-25 years of age have received non-renewal notices from their insurers — a stressful and financially disruptive situation. Installing a new, properly permitted, and HVHZ-compliant roof through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing not only keeps you insurable but positions you to shop for better rates with confidence. Our team can provide guidance on the wind mitigation process and help ensure your new roof is documented in a way that maximizes your insurance benefit.

faqs.q: Does my Hollywood home really need an HVHZ-rated roofing system, or is that just for new construction?
faqs.a: Yes, absolutely. Any permitted roofing project in Hollywood — whether a full replacement or a substantial repair — must use products that hold Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) approval, as required by the Florida Building Code for High Velocity Hurricane Zone properties. This applies to both new construction and existing homes undergoing roof replacement. Using non-approved materials in Hollywood is a code violation that can result in failed inspections, insurance claim denials, and serious legal liability.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement typically cost in Hollywood, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: A full roof replacement in Hollywood typically ranges from $12,000 to $20,000 for architectural shingles and $18,000 to $35,000 for tile systems, depending on the size, pitch, and complexity of the roof. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible to upgrade your roof today without a large upfront investment. We'll provide a detailed, no-obligation written estimate and walk you through all available financing options during your consultation.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Hollywood, Florida, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Hollywood require a permit issued by the City of Hollywood Building Division, and the work must pass a formal inspection before the permit can be closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf — we prepare and submit all required documentation, coordinate with the Building Division, and schedule inspections at the appropriate project milestones. You won't have to navigate the permitting bureaucracy on your own; we handle it as part of every job.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Hollywood, and what should I expect during the process?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements in Hollywood are completed within two to four days of active installation, depending on the size of the home, the material selected, and weather conditions. The overall project timeline — from signed contract to final permit inspection — typically runs two to four weeks when accounting for permitting and material lead times. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will provide you with a clear project schedule upfront, keep you informed at every stage, and minimize disruption to your daily routine throughout the process.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Hollywood, and by how much?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. After installing a new, HVHZ-compliant roof, Hollywood homeowners are eligible for a Wind Mitigation Inspection, which documents hurricane-resistant features for your insurance carrier. Based on the results, many carriers apply wind mitigation credits that reduce the wind portion of your annual premium by 20% to 45% or more. For Hollywood homeowners paying $4,000 to $8,000 or more annually in homeowner's insurance, this can translate to $1,500 to $4,000 in annual savings — a compelling financial return on your roofing investment.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Hollywood Home with a Hurricane-Rated Roof? Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Is Ready.
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof inspection and estimate — and discover why hundreds of Hollywood homeowners trust The Kings of Service to protect everything they've built.
